---
title: "Find a Gene Project"
author: "Ricardo Carias (PID: A18573289)"
format: html
toc: TRUE 
---

## Heat Map 

```{r}
library(bio3d)

# read alignment
LDHB_Align <- read.fasta("FASTAmuscle-I20260228-224224-0678-26581557-p1m.aln-fasta")

# calculate sequence identity matrix
id <- seqidentity(LDHB_Align)

# generate heatmap
heatmap(id,
        symm = TRUE,
        margins = c(8,8))
```



```{r}
library(bio3d)

# read the AlphaFold alignment file (this is the MSA generated by AF)
# we are importing it as a fasta alignment object
aln <- read.fasta("test_e07e8.a3m copy")

# extract just the alignment characters (the actual letters)
# and convert them into a matrix where:
# rows = sequences
# columns = alignment positions
aln_mat <- as.matrix(aln$ali)

# now we calculate conservation per column
# apply(..., 2, ...) means:
# apply this function across columns (MARGIN = 2)
conservation <- apply(aln_mat, 2, function(col) {
  
  # remove gaps from this column so we don’t count "-"
  col <- col[col != "-"]
  
  # if a column is entirely gaps, give it conservation = 0
  if(length(col) == 0) return(0)
  
  # table(col) counts how many times each amino acid appears
  # max(table(col)) finds the most common residue count
  # dividing by length(col) converts that into a fraction
  # this gives percent conservation for that column
  max(table(col)) / length(col)
})

# set a conservation threshold (80% in this case)
# meaning: at least 80% of sequences share the same residue
cutoff <- 0.8

# identify which alignment positions meet that threshold
conserved_positions <- which(conservation >= cutoff)

# print those positions
conserved_positions
```


```{r}
library(bio3d)

# read the AlphaFold alignment file generated by ColabFold
aln <- read.fasta("test_e07e8.a3m copy")

# extract the alignment characters and convert to matrix
# rows = sequences
# columns = alignment positions
aln_mat <- as.matrix(aln$ali)

# compute conservation score for each alignment column
# apply(..., 2, ...) applies the function across columns
conservation <- apply(aln_mat, 2, function(col) {

  # remove gap characters
  col <- col[col != "-"]

  # if column contains only gaps return 0
  if(length(col) == 0) return(0)

  # calculate fraction of most frequent amino acid
  max(table(col)) / length(col)
})

# define conservation cutoff (80%)
cutoff <- 0.8

# identify positions meeting conservation threshold
conserved_positions <- which(conservation >= cutoff)

# print conserved residue positions
conserved_positions
```
